metadata.author: David Ancor
metadata.stakeholders: Founders; Product Managers; Engineering Teams; Team Admins; Compliance Officers; Site Reliability / DevOps
metadata.parent_okr: Ship c1v v1.0 with metric-traceable specs and live feedback loop
metadata.regulatory_refs: SOC 2 Type II; HIPAA; GDPR; PCI-DSS (scope tbd in Module 4)

use_case_name: UC04 — Emit CLI Commands from Spec
initial_conditions: An approved spec exists (state 'approved' produced by UC03).
The Engineering Team Member is authenticated (cross-cutting UC14).
The Engineering Team Member holds emit permission on the spec's organization.
At least one IDE/CLI client is registered for the Engineering Team Member's account.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member requests a CLI bundle for an approved spec

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall authenticate the Engineering Team Member's session before accepting the emission request (CC.R01).
The system shall verify the referenced spec's status is exactly 'approved' before proceeding.
The system shall verify the Engineering Team Member holds emit permission on the spec's organization.
The system shall load the approved spec bundle at its committed hash from version-controlled storage.
The system shall generate a SKILL.md export from the spec's agent and capability sections.
The system shall generate a CLAUDE.md export from the spec's conventions and architecture sections.
The system shall generate one MCP tool definition for each interface declared in the spec's interface matrix (seed from Module 6).
The system shall package the SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, and MCP tool definitions into a single CLI bundle.
The system shall sign the CLI bundle with the spec's commit hash and the emission timestamp.
The system shall complete the CLI bundle generation within CLI_EMISSION_TIMEOUT_SEC of the emission request.

actor_steps_table.extra_actor_col: Version Control receives the signed CLI bundle and records the commit reference.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall write the signed CLI bundle to the spec's Version Control repository under the emission path.
The system shall deliver the signed CLI bundle to the Engineering Team Member's registered IDE/CLI client.

actor_steps_table.other_actors: IDE/CLI client acknowledges receipt of the CLI bundle with a receipt token.

actor_steps_table.the_system: The system shall require an IDE/CLI receipt token before marking the emission as delivered.
The system shall record an audit entry for the CLI emission with Engineering Team Member identity, spec commit hash, bundle signature, IDE/CLI client identifier, and timestamp (CC.R02).
The system shall return the bundle location, signature, and commit hash to the Engineering Team Member.

actor_steps_table.primary_actor: Engineering Team Member receives the bundle location and signature

ending_conditions: A signed CLI bundle containing SKILL.md, CLAUDE.md, and one MCP tool definition per interface is delivered to the Engineering Team Member's IDE/CLI client.
The signed CLI bundle is written to the spec's Version Control repository under the emission path.
The emission is recorded in the audit trail with Engineering Team Member identity, spec commit hash, bundle signature, IDE/CLI client identifier, and timestamp.
The Engineering Team Member holds the bundle location, signature, and commit hash.
notes: Scope boundary: UC04 covers the emission of a CLI bundle from one approved spec to one IDE/CLI client. Multi-client fan-out is a separate invocation per client.
Bundle format is standardized as SKILL.md + CLAUDE.md + MCP tool definitions to satisfy the M1 hard constraint 'no single-vendor lock-in' — multi-IDE contract.
<<extends>> UC01 per inventory: UC04 runs automatically after UC03 approval only if auto-emit is enabled in the organization; otherwise UC04 is invoked on-demand.
Signing uses spec commit hash + emission timestamp so any IDE/CLI client can verify the bundle without calling back into c1v.
Version Control write (step 13) creates an auditable emission log separate from the in-product audit trail (step 17); both must succeed for the emission to be marked complete.
Cross-cutting: session auth (CC.R01), audit trail (CC.R02), RBAC on emit permission (CC.R03).

Degradation: if Version Control write fails, the system retries up to MAX_RETRIES and surfaces the failure to the Engineering Team Member; the IDE/CLI delivery leg is independent and may still succeed.
MCP tool definition count depends on the spec's interface matrix (from Module 6). First-pass v1 assumes a minimum of one MCP tool per spec.
